steps.label: What we want to prove
steps.explanation: Given P and P → Q, prove Q. This is modus ponens — the most fundamental rule of logic. It has been known since antiquity.

steps.label: State our premises
steps.explanation: We have two premises: P (a fact) and P → Q (an implication). These are the leaves of our proof tree.

steps.label: Apply →-Elim (Modus Ponens)
steps.explanation: →-Elimination says: if you have P and P → Q, you can conclude Q. The implication 'fires' — we feed P into P → Q and get Q out. This is also called modus ponens.
